caption.court: HUD, U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development, Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity
caption.caption_note: Retaliation complaint anchored to HACC core housing policies

parties.plaintiff: Aggrieved person / complainant (name to be inserted).
parties.defendant: Housing Authority of Clackamas County (HACC), respondent.

jurisdiction_and_venue: This draft is structured as an administrative housing-discrimination intake for HUD and should be tailored to the final statutory basis asserted.
HUD jurisdiction and timeliness should be confirmed against the final incident dates, protected-basis theory, and requested relief.
The dispute appears to arise from housing-program administration, adverse action, and procedural protections connected to HACC operations.
factual_allegations: The complaint centers on Retaliation complaint anchored to HACC core housing policies.
The complainant contends that the grievance or hearing process was not handled in the manner described by HACC policy.
The complainant contends that appeal rights and due-process protections were not clearly honored.
The complainant contends that HACC moved toward denial or termination of assistance without clear notice and documented process.

Timeline detail from intake: 72 information_extraction: 0.74 empathy: 0.68 efficiency: 0.69 coverage: 0.73 FEEDBACK: Good coverage, but the mediator can ask more specific timeline and witness questions.
The core documentary support for these allegations appears in Exhibit A (ADMISSIONS AND CONTINUED OCCUPANCY POLICY) and Exhibit B (ADMINISTRATIVE PLAN).
claims_theory: Likely authority implicated by the current theory includes Fair Housing Act anti-retaliation provisions, 24 C.F.R. Part 100.
HACC appears to have pursued or upheld a denial or termination of assistance without a clearly documented and transparent adverse-action process.

The available policy language suggests the complainant should have received an informal review or hearing, written notice, and a review decision, but the intake narrative describes those protections as missing or unclear.
The complainant also describes a retaliation theory based on the timing of the adverse treatment after protected complaints or grievance activity.

The strongest policy support for these theories is: 14 GRIEVANCES AND APPEALS INTRODUCTION ......................................................................................................................14-1 PART I: INFORMAL HEARINGS FOR PUBLIC HOUSING APPLICANTS I.A. OVERVIEW ...................................................................................................................14-1 INFOR... causes_of_action.title: Administrative Fair Housing Process Failure
causes_of_action.theory: The draft facts suggest denial or termination activity without the clear notice, review, or hearing process described by HACC policy.
support: Likely authority implicated by the current theory includes Fair Housing Act anti-retaliation provisions, 24 C.F.R. Part 100.
HACC appears to have pursued or upheld a denial or termination of assistance without a clearly documented and transparent adverse-action process.
Documentary support: Exhibit B (ADMINISTRATIVE PLAN). Rationale: selected for stronger notice and process language.

proposed_allegations: Complainant alleges conduct arising from Retaliation complaint anchored to HACC core housing policies.
The intake record suggests a dispute involving grievance hearing.
The intake record suggests a dispute involving appeal rights.
The intake record suggests a dispute involving adverse action.

During intake, the complainant stated that I reported discrimination to human resources after my supervisor denied a promotion and made repeated comments about women not being fit for leadership; Two days later I was terminated.

requested_relief: Administrative investigation of the challenged housing practices and adverse-action process.

Any additional relief HUD is authorized to obtain or recommend.
